_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that scientists collect information through experiments and observations to explain how nature works.

_source.w: We are learning that scientists collect information through experiments and observations to explain how nature works.
y: This helps us understand that scientific knowledge comes from careful investigation, not just guessing.
Knowing how scientists work helps us think critically about claims people make about the world.
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples have gathered knowledge through careful observation of Country over thousands of generations.
eg: Scientists observing how tadpoles change into frogs over many weeks.
A scientist measuring how fast different plants grow in sun versus shade.
Researchers counting the number of birds in a wetland each year to track changes.
Scientists using thermometers to record ocean temperatures over time.
Marie Curie carrying out experiments to discover new elements.
Ecologists observing how fire helps some Australian plants grow new shoots.
